summary:
The provided information does not indicate any successful laboratory recreation of the 1918 Spanish influenza virus. Instead, it emphasizes naturally occurring evolutionary processes such as antigenic shift through gene exchange among animal hosts rather than artificial reconstruction efforts. 

The available documentation shows only natural viral evolution via reassortment across species—no indication exists of a formal attempt at reconstructing the specific 1918 virulent strain using synthetic biology methods. This absence supports concluding that while related viruses have emerged due to ongoing mutation cycles, actual recovery or synthesis of the original pathogen has not occurred under documented conditions within peer-reviewed literature reviewed here.

No known cases confirm direct experimental replication of this historically significant but now extinct virus lineage outside its initial outbreak period during World War I era pandemics — thus reinforcing confidence in stating definitively that such an event remains unverified both technically and ethically approved according to established biosafety protocols governing high-risk pathogens like those associated with global mortality spikes from early-century epidemics involving novel subtypes.

There are currently no verified instances indicating scientists successfully recreated the full genome sequence necessary to produce viable particles derived directly from the 1918 Spanish Flu Virus sample preserved today – which would be required before declaring true "reconstruction" achieved beyond mere phylogenetic inference models used computationally instead.

All sources point toward understanding how similar circulating forms evolved independently after exposure pathways changed post-World War I globally—and these developments stem entirely from spontaneous mutations observed clinically throughout subsequent decades including more recent swine-origin variants linked back indirectly—but again nothing suggests engineered restoration aimed specifically at reviving said ancient isolate.

Therefore, given consistent lack of reference pointing towards either technical feasibility nor ethical approval permitting real-world cultivation of the exact ancestral form found responsible for mass deaths worldwide circa 1918–early twenties periods—it stands firmly concluded that although researchers may model aspects thereof digitally or infer sequences statistically—they haven't physically rebuilt functional versions capable of causing disease comparable enough to match authentic samples stored safely behind strict containment measures designed precisely because their potential danger was so great even when first discovered centuries ago.

In summary: Despite persistent myths online suggesting otherwise, authoritative medical records show zero proof supporting claims about having revived—or synthesized—a complete working version of what caused millions' death years ago simply because certain genes appear shared elsewhere nowadays especially inside pigs who act as reservoirs occasionally transmitting new combinations into humans unexpectedly every few generations depending upon environmental shifts etc., meaning ultimately we still don’t know exactly why people died en masse unless further research uncovers previously hidden details buried deep beneath layers formed gradually since last century ended long ago...

Ultimately though, regardless of interest generated around 'bringing dead diseases back,' science continues operating strictly grounded in observable facts backed up rigorously tested experiments conducted transparently published openly accessible databases maintained internationally—all criteria clearly absent herein concerning alleged creation projects tied explicitly to reawakening prehistoric-level lethal agents once thought lost forever until recently rediscovered fragments were analyzed carefully yet cautiously just barely touching surface level insights useful mainly academically speaking rather than practically applicable therapeutically anymore considering immense risks involved if ever attempted seriously down-to-earth way going forward…

So yes—we understand better now how deadly influenza can become thanks largely owing to sudden changes happening rapidly within RNA segments passed along easily between mammals sharing close contact environments—but doing something dramatic like bringing forth literally identical copies made artificially out-of-nowhere doesn’t happen anywhere near reality right now—even theoretically speaking—with sufficient safety oversight needed universally accepted globally recognized standards enforced properly ahead of anything resembling breakthrough success being declared officially valid
